evidence_quotes.quote: dark pattern system set forth by the seller.

paragraphs.text: I believe this act would be the perfect opportunity to address payment models, as well as payment processing platforms. Traditionally, any good or services required an exchange of physical payment in order to take place. This gave the customer the opportunity to refuse payment whenever they wanted to terminate a service or did not want to finalize a purchase. In the digital world, this power has been taken away from the customer and allocated to financial institutions and payment processors. Almost all digital payments happen through the intervention of financial institutions and are processed by either Visa or Mastercard. This relationship easily facilitates an abusive environment for consumers, because now they must rely on multiple, independent, for-profit businesses in order to conduct their daily lives on the internet. Subsequently, these institutions can now dictate what can or can not be sold by refusing service to a seller for perfectly legal goods, because of arbitrary reasons that the financial institutions and payment processors have set forth. This is akin to cartel behavior, but for the digital world. I believe consumers would greatly benefit by having this authority moved back into their hands. This would be achieved by updating policy to hold financial institutions responsible to ensure that once a consumer no longer wants a service, the transaction can no longer take place, thus eliminating the consumer's need to comply with a dark pattern system set forth by the seller. Likewise, it the duopoly of Visa and Mastercard has to be addressed. These two companies currently have overwhelming control of digital markets. Their service should be classified as a basic need for society. Much like electricity and water are the minimum decency requirement for living in a physical home in the 21st century, payment processing has become a minimum requirement for living in the digital world. In order for consumers to be properly protected, there can not be the risk of revoked service from payment processors, which would effectively render someone unable to sell to most of the digital market. Thank you for your consideration.
